mechanism_narrative: The year-over-year growth rate of industrial production (INDPRO) positively correlates with S&P 500 forward 20-day returns, reflecting real economic momentum that supports corporate earnings and market valuations.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO YoY change with 20-day forward return remains below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals a weakening labor market, which historically predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as consumer spending falters and recession risks increase.
failure_condition: If forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When both the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 2.5% and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is below 3%, the real policy rate is negative, indicating accommodative monetary policy that supports equity valuations and predicts positive 20-day returns.
regime_hint: inflation above target but policy accommodative
failure_condition: If the rank IC between the joint signal and 20-day forward returns is less than 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During early expansion phases when GDP growth is above 3%, rising housing starts (HOUST) act as a leading indicator of further economic strength, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon. The effect is regime-specific to early expansions.
failure_condition: If in early expansion periods the correlation between HOUST month-over-month change and 20-day forward returns is not positive with p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly retail sales (RSAFS) provide a real-time gauge of consumer spending, and positive month-over-month growth consistently signals strong aggregate demand, leading to positive short-term equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the Sharpe ratio of a long position when RSAFS MoM growth is positive is negative over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in payroll employment (PAYEMS 3-month change >0.5%) and industrial production (INDPRO 3-month change >0.5%) indicates broad-based economic synchronization, which historically produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days as corporate earnings exceed expectations.
failure_condition: If the average forward 20-day return during these periods is not significantly greater than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate larger than 25 basis points signals a tightening monetary policy cycle, which historically depresses equity valuations via higher discount rates, leading to negative forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: If the mean forward 20-day return after such rate increases is non-negative over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a full employment regime (UNRATE below 4%), a three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.1 percentage point is a more potent recession warning than similar moves in higher unemployment regimes, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days due to rapid labor market deterioration.
failure_condition: If the average forward 60-day return in triggered instances within the full employment regime is not significantly negative (one-sided test p > 0.05).
